entry.title: Joule integration with SAP Signavio Process Transformation Suite – Setup Guide
entry.content:
Introduction:

While SAP Signavio Process Transformation Suite helps organizations enhance operational efficiency, drive innovation, and achieve strategic business objectives, we are happy to introduce SAP Copilot–Joule. With Joule integration, you will be able to interact with your system using Joule patterns such as Informational, Transactional, and Navigational scenarios, and leverage multiple capabilities from SAP Signavio. You will be able to interact with:

  • SAP Signavio Process Collaboration Hub
  • SAP Signavio Journey Modeler
  • SAP Signavio Process Intelligence
  • Value accelerator library for SAP Signavio solutions

Before we get started with the Joule integration with SAP Signavio, it’s important to understand the overall Joule integration for your organization. Please look at the blogs below:

This blog post is a series for Joule Activation:

  • SAP Business AI – Overview for all !!! (Includes information on Joule Supported Data Centers, Different scenarios during Joule setup, etc..)
  • Joule - Unified Setup: Bridging Simplicity and Performance (Demo Video to understand the Unified Setup, Joule basics & architecture, how to set up Joule, etc...) 
  • Joule for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition - A Comprehensive Setup Guide
  • Joule Setup – End-to-End Setup Guide (for all Line of Business) Unified Approach 
  • Joule for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition - Setup Guide
  • SAP Digital Supply Chain
    • Joule for SAP Integrated Business Planning (SAP IBP) – Setup Guide
    • Joule Setup for SAP Integrated Product Development
  • Joule – Getting Started with Document Grounding - setup guide
  • Activate Joule with SAP Build Work Zone and SAP Mobile Start
  • Analytical Insights
    • Analytical Insights in Joule – Setup Guide
  • Joule Integration with SAP Ariba - Setup Guide
  • Joule Integration with SAP Concur - Setup Guide

👉SAP Signavio - you are here

Blogs - Work in Progress (WIP):

  • SAP Digital Supply Chain (WIP)
    • SAP Asset Performance Management
    • SAP Product Lifecycle Management
    • SAP Digital Manufacturing
  • and more to be added towards General Availability

****************************************************************************

It is always recommended to check the SAP Official Help Page for Joule Integration with Signavio Solutions and check the Joule Capabilities (user interactions with Signavio).  

To begin with, let us take a look at the prerequisites for Joule integration with SAP Signavio:

  • You have your SAP Signavio Process Transformation Suite
  • Joule Entitlements in your SAP Business Technology Platform - In case you do not have the Joule license you can opt for the 0$ order from SAP Store also be familiar with Joule Base and Premium capabilities 
  • SAP Cloud Identity Services
  • SAP Build Work Zone (planned for next release,  not required for now)

While we plan your Joule integration, it’s important to know your landscape and how you would like to activate Joule.

Image 1

We will explain both the processes, with Section 1 focusing on first-time setup and Section 2 focusing on Joule with an existing setup, to make it easy for our customers. For both these sections, SAP Cloud Identity Service setup is common with your SAP Signavio systems.  

Pre-requisites for Joule activation with SAP Signavio:

  • SAP Signavio
  • SAP Business Technology Platform with Joule Entitlements
  • SAP Cloud Identity Services is working with your SAP Signavio system

 

1. SAP Signavio with SAP Cloud Identity Services

To activate Joule in SAP Signavio, we first need to ensure we have the prerequisites. Let's look at the details of configuring SAP Cloud Identity Services for your system.

Note: In case you already have SAP Cloud Identity configured with SAP Signavio, you may skip this section.

Image 2

To achieve the above setup, you will need access as an Administrator to your SAP Cloud Identity Services and your SAP Signavio system.

We highly recommend checking the Single Sign-on Using SAML help page and the following sections

- Set Up SSO Using SAML

- Configure Your IdP

- Enabling SSO Using SAML

- Invite New Users by Email

- Enforce SSO to Disable Login with Credentials

The details below can guide one of the processes to Setup SSO with SAP Cloud Identity Services.

 

1.1 Download the Metadata file from SAP Cloud Identity Services

This file is required to exchange the SAML 2.0 files and establish trust between the systems. More information is available here. 

 

⚠️Ensure you have selected the same SAP Cloud Identity used across the landscape for SSO. This is essential for the best user experience with Joule. For more details, please visit our previous blog (Joule—Unified Setup: Bridging Simplicity and Performance) and ensure you use the same Domain Name as in your SAP Cloud Identity Services. Note that the Cloud Identity Service tenant cannot vary in case you want to connect multiple solutions to Joule.

Log in to your SAP Cloud Identity Services tenant, click on Applications and Resources, select Tenant Settings -> select Single Sign-On -> click on SAML 2.0 Configurations, and click on Download Metadata File.

Image 3

We shall go ahead and import this metadata file into you SAP Signavio System.

 

1.2 Establish Trust and Download the Metadata file from SAP Signavio

Log in to your SAP Signavio system, click on the App Menu icon, and click on Process Manager.

Image 4

In the Process Manager page, click on Setup and click on Manage SAP Signavio Process Collaboration Hub authentication as shown below.

Image 5

In the new window, you will have a few options to activate Single Sign-on Using SAML. You may refer to the section Enable SSO Using SAML. You will need to select the following options:

  • Enable SAML 2.0 authentication
  • Allow service provider-initiated authentication
  • Sign authentication request
  • Create new user accounts automatically (this will be an optional parameter, please validate before you select it)
  • Copy the code from your SAP Cloud Identity Services metadata file and paste it into the Metadata section, as shown below

Click on Download the SAML service provider metadata, now this Metadata will be added to the SAP Cloud Identity Services

Image 6

Once you have validated the details, Save it.

1.3 Create an Application for Signavio in SAP Cloud Identity Services

We will be creating a new Application in your SAP Cloud Identity Services to setup a SAML trust between the systems. You may also refer to the official SAP Note - 3461963 for more details.

In your SAP Cloud Identity Services, click on Application & Resources -> click on Applications -> then click on Create.

 

Image 7

In the Create Application, please enter the details to recognize your system. You may follow the details as shown below and Save the settings.

  • Display Name – Signavio System
  • Type: SAP Signavio Solution
  • Protocol Type: SAML 2.0

Image 8

A new application will be created. Please click on the new application you created for Signavio System, click on SAML 2.0 Configuration, click on Define from Metadata, click on Browse in the new window, and upload it.

Image 9

You should be able to see the Metadata file information once it’s uploaded as shown below.

Image 10

In the same section of your application Signavio System, click on Subject Name Identifier change the Identity Directory - Value to Global User ID, and Save the settings.

 

Image 11

Next, we will need to add a value in the Attributes section. Please click on Attributes and click on Add and ensure you add the attribute Name as “signavio_licenses_v1” and the Value in my case I have “Enterprise Plus Edition”  (You can choose your license type - The name of the license that you want to assign to a user, for example "Enterprise Plus Edition", "Enterprise Edition", "Classic Edition", "Collaboration Hub", or "Workflow". If required you can also refer to the note3461963 - Configuring SSO with SAP IAS for SAP Signavio).

Before you save the details, please validate the other details such as email, first_name, last_name, and user_uuid as per the screen, and Save it. 

 

Image 12

In case you want to add more values please look at Configure your IdP section on the help page.

This completes the bidirectional trust between the SAP Signavio system and the SAP Cloud Identity Services.

 

1.4 Test your SAP Signavio SSO with SAP Cloud Identity Services

To access your SAP Signavio system with SSO you can use the following URL syntax:

https://<<ServerRegion>>.signavio.com/p/hub?t=<WORKSPACE_ID> 

In case you do not know your WORKSPACE_ID, within your SAP Signavio system click on the question icon and you can find the Workspace ID as shown below.

 

Image 13

So, in my case, my SAP Signavio SSO URL is:

https://editor.signavio.com/p/hub?t=5xxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxc

Once I launch this URL, SAP Signavio auto-redirects to the SAP Cloud Identity Services page for Authentication, and users should be able to log in successfully.

Further, you can check the roles and authorization management here - Manage Users and Access Rights.

 

1.5 SAP Cloud Identity Services Additional Configurations

This is an additional process that will allow you to differentiate the access for Base and Premium users. To achieve this setup, we will create 3 different groups.

  • grp_joule_skills_personal_data_restricted used for Personally Identifiable Information (PII)
  • sap.sig.base
  • sap.sig.premium

Note:

  • I recommend reading the help page “SAP Cloud Identity Services Additional Configurations” for more information on each role.
  • A base role is required for all users. If users need Premium and/or PII skills, please create the groups and maintain the users, respectively.

I am showing Base user group creations; you can follow the same for the others. You can navigate to SAP Cloud Identity Services, click on Users and Authentication, click on Groups, click on Create, enter the following details, and click on Next Step.

Image 14

Once you enter the details, click on Next Step (Optional: Search for the Joule integration; you can skip this application selection as it’s not mandatory).

Image 15

Review the details, then click Finish.

Image 16

Once the group is created, you can add the users as required.

Image 17

Ensure to repeat these steps for the entire group and confirm the required authorization.

 

 

2. Preparing for Joule Integration

Once you have the SAP Signavio system ready, please ensure you align with the Unified Cross User Experience approach. Then, plan to either use an existing SAP BTP Subaccount or create a new one only if this is your organization's first project. It should be one Joule for all your SAP applications.

Image 18

Case 1: If you are doing the Joule setup for the very first time in your organization, then please refer to this blog to create your SAP BTP Subaccount and run the Joule Booster - Joule Setup – End-to-End Setup Guide (for all Line of Business) Unified Approach.

  • In the current release of Joule with SAP Signavio, you may skip the steps related to SAP Build Work Zone, as they are not required.

Case 2: If your organization already has an active Joule setup and using the same SAP Cloud Identity Services, then you can add the SAP Signavio system to the existing formations as shown below.

3. Adding SAP Signavio to an existing Joule formation

Formation is a logical grouping of SAP systems together. In Joule case, if the booster has been executed previously, we can use the same formation to integrate the SAP Signavio system.

Note: Please check if the existing formation is connected to Test or Production systems and plan to use them or create a new SAP BTP Subaccount.

To use the existing SAP BTP formations, navigate to SAP BTP GA, click on System Landscape, click on Formations, and search for the existing formation to which you want to add the SAP Signavio system.

In the formation below, you can see that we have a formation in a Ready state, and SAP Signavio is not part of it. Click on Include System.

Image 19

If you have too many systems, filter it out by SAP Signavio or else select the SAP Signavio system and click on Next.

Image 20

Select both options, as we want Joule to support SAP Signavio scenarios and enable the Joule icon in the SAP Signavio system, click on Review.

Image 21

Once you verify the details, click on Include.

Image 22

Once you click on Include, the formation status changes to Synchronizing, and it might take 5-10 minutes at times to become Ready.

Image 23

3.1 Post checks in your SAP BTP Subaccount

Once your SAP Signavio is set with Joule either via Booster or the Include System in Formation, you should be able to see the settings in your Subaccount.

  • An instance is created, which can be found in Instances and Subscriptions

Image 24

  • A SIGNAVIO_SSO Destination is created as shown below

Image 25

If these things show up, we can continue to set up the additional destinations required.

4. Creating SAP Signavio Destination in the SAP BTP Subaccount

To complete the setup, we need to set up two more destinations - SIGNAVIO_WZ_CONFIG and SIGNAVIO_WZ_RUNTIME to use the navigation service. 

Note:  SAP Build Work Zone integration will be released soon and will be automated soon; however, it is a manual setup with this release and we use the destination names to sync with next releases. 

4.1 Activate Destination Service and Create a Service Key

To create the destination service, we will need to activate services called Destination Service in your SAP BTP Subaccount. Navigate to our Joule subaccount, click on Services -> click on Service Marketplace - > look for a service called Destination Service -> and click dots and click Create.

Image 26

In the new pop-up screen, you will be able to see the default values that will be picked up based on your setup. In my case, it’s using Cloud Foundry and the Space from the previous setup and you have to give the Instance Name and click on Create.

Image 27

Once the instance is created you should be able to see the details in the Instance and Subscription section under Instances.

 

 

 Image 28

Click on the three-dots icon for your cli-signavio instance, select Create Service Key, enter the details as shown below, and click on Create.

Image 29

Once the Service Key is created download this file. You can click either view or download the file as we need the values to create the destinations. We will need the ClientID, Client Secret, URL, and the URI from this file.

Image 30

4.2 Create Destinations

To create the SIGNAVIO_WZ_CONFIG and SIGNAVIO_WZ_RUNTIME, click on Connectivity  -> click on Destinations ->, and click on Create Destination.

  • We will set the SIGNAVIO_WZ_CONFIG destination as table below

Field

Value

Name

SIGNAVIO_WZ_CONFIG

Type

HTTP

Description

Destination Service Configuration

URL

Portal-service URI Value from the service key created for the service instance of SAP Signavio (paste here the address you copied step in 7, then add the suffix /destination-configuration/v1/destinations/).

Proxy Type

Internet

Authentication

OAuth2UserTokenExchange

Client ID

Client ID from the service key created for the service instance of SAP Signavio (located in the JSON file, see step 7)

Client Secret

Client Secret from the service key created for the service instance of SAP Signavio (located in the JSON file, see step 7)

Token Service URL Type

Dedicated

Token Service URL

Enter the URL to the OAuth2 token service in the following format:

https://<my-subdomain>.authentication.<region>.hana.ondemand.com/oauth/token

Additional Properties

Verify that the checkbox Use default JDK trust store is selected

 

The destination should be like this.

Image 31

Save the Destination if all is good.

  • Create the second destination for SIGNAVIO_WZ_RUNTIME

Field

Value

Name

SIGNAVIO_WZ_RUNTIME

Type

HTTP

Description

Signavio WZ Runtime URL

URL

Base URL of your Signavio client from your Signavio workspace, example: https://editor.signavio.com or https://app-us.signavio.com validate your Signavio URL

Proxy Type

Internet

Authentication

NoAuthentication

Additional Properties

Verify that the checkbox Use default JDK trust store is selected

 

The destination should look like below.

Image 32

This completes the setup of Joule in SAP Signavio.

 

5. Test SAP Signavio with Joule

Now let’s log in to the SAP Signavio system and launch the Joule icon to test the functionalities.

Image 33

For more information on the Joule capabilities and how it works with SAP Signavio please check our official SAP help page.

 

Credits & Special thanks to the SAP Signavio Team (Stafen Thomas & Andreas Hasler) & RIG Team (Marvin Klose & Amritha Varshini) for supporting this blog post. 

Stay tuned for more updates!!! Happy Learning for now!!!

Regards,
Nagesh Caparthy
SAP Business AI RIG Team

 

 

 

Troubleshooting IAS Issues:

- Joule Blank screen:

Reason: Due to IAS domain mismatch or the URL is not part of the trusted domain in BTP cockpit and SAP Cloud Identity Services.

Fix:

  • Check if the SAP Signavio URL is trusted in your BTP Subaccount
  • Check if the SAP Signavio URL is trusted in your SAP Cloud Identity Services
  • Check if you are using the same Metadata URL from your SAP Cloud Identity Services as per the subaccount trust setup – if you have used ondemand.com vs cloud.sap in your subaccount the metadata from SAP Cloud Identity should also match the same.

entry.title: SAP Codejam on Event Driven Build Process
entry.content:
I recently participated in an SAP Code Jam focused on building event-driven processes using SAP Integration Suite (Advanced Event Mesh) and SAP Build Process Automation. The Session was ably conducted and managed by @ajay_soreng  and @Rekha_DR .

Codejams are my favorite community events. It pure code with access to the best of SAP in terms of tools, license and expertise.  In this session we had a hands-on and walkthrough of a realistic end-to-end scenario rather than a theoretical demo.

The core use case was triggered by a Business Partner creation event in a simulated SAP S/4HANA Cloud system. Once the Business Partner was created and tagged, an event was published to SAP Advanced Event Mesh, where I configured topics, queues, and subscriptions to reliably route the event.

As you see in the below soln. diagram, an event is triggered by App. The advanced event mesh is configured to handle these events and appropriately route it to the build process and finally allow user to act on it. 

Event Triggered from a Mock UI: 

Configure the Broker Manager - The best part of this code jam:

The plethora of options of Event mesh were made available to us. The Queues, RDPs, Connectors, Tyr me's etc. was a learning experience.  

As for the use case, 

using a REST Delivery Point, the event was forwarded from Advanced Event Mesh to SAP Build Process Automation, which automatically triggered a business process. From there, the flow combined event-driven automation with human interaction—a user approval step in the SAP Build Inbox—followed by an asynchronous call to a simulated third-party badge system.

The process paused, waited for a callback, and then resumed to complete the flow with a final notification and badge ID.

 

 

What stood out most was seeing how cleanly systems were decoupled. The event producer had no awareness of the consumers, and SAP Build acted purely as an orchestrator reacting to events. Configuring and testing this live made the benefits of event-driven architecture very tangible.

Overall, I enjoyed the Code Jam as it was a great practical deep dive into how SAP Advanced Event Mesh and SAP Build Process Automation.

Thanks to all the developer advocates, especially @ajmaradiaga for compiling the content on integration suite and adv. event mesh.

Until the next code jam - Ciao !

entry.title: Re: Deletion of CI_COBL
entry.content:
Hi again,

This post is a continuation of my previous post about deleting CI_COBL.
This time, I tested the process on a development system, not a demo environment, and I will share my experience step by step.

What did we do in short?

In the previous work, we did the following:

* We added a custom field starting with ZZ* to the CI_COBL include structure.

* No value was assigned to this field anywhere in the system.

* The work was never moved outside the development environment.

Important:

CI_COBL only contained the field we added.
Because there were no other custom fields, we wanted to remove CI_COBL entirely.

No problems were found during testing in the demo system.
Then, we decided to do it in the development system.

Deletion Process on the Development System

I carried out this process late at night.

First, to ensure no one was working on the system:

* I logged all users out of the system.

* I locked logon access.

Then I started the deletion steps.

Step 1 – Deleting CI_COBL

First:

SE11 → Data Type → CI_COBL → Delete

The deletion process took about 15 minutes.

During this time, I checked via ST22 and saw that several inconsistency dumps had occurred.

The dumps originated from RFCUSER and my own user.

Step 2 – CDS Activation Errors

After the deletion, some CDS views did not activate.

I got these errors:

DDL source V_COEPACDOCA_AGE could not be activated
DDL source V_COEP_ACDOCA_DDL could not be activated
DDL source V_COEP_ACDOCA_R1 could not be activated
Most recent runtime object DTDC FINS_DC_ACDOCA_GL could not be read
DDL source NSDM_DDL_MSEG could not be activated
DDL source V_COEP could not be activated
DDL source V_COVP_KAEP could not be activated
DDL source V_COVP_KAEP_11P could not be activated
DDL source V_COEP_V2_4 could not be activated
DDL source V_COVP_WOTP could not be activated

NSDM_DDL_MSEG Problem

There was a previously made extend view via ADT.
I deleted it and MSEG became active again.

Solution for V_COEP* CDS Views

To fix the V_COEP* CDS views, I ran the following program:

FCO_CDS_VIEW_GENERATE

iv_no_check = X
iv_reset = X **I set these parameters to X in debug mode.

After running the program, all V_COEP views were activated successfully.

I also checked it in SQL, and it worked fine.

Step 3 – Testing Basic Operations

To check for issues, I ran some key transactions:
F-02, FB05, FB03, MIGO, MB5B

I received no errors.

OData Service Control

I also reactivated some services that were inactive because of the CI_COBL field:

FAC_MANAGE_POSTING_TMPL_SRV
FAC_FOREIGN_VAL_POST_SRV
FAP_MANUAL_CLEARING_SRV
FAR_PAYMENT_POST_SRV
FAC_GL_DOCUMENT_POST_SRV
FAR_DOWN_PAYMENT_REQUEST_MANAGE_SRV
FAC_FINANCIAL_DOCUMENT_SRV_01
FAR_BS_ITM_REPROC_SRV
FAC_GL_MANUAL_CLEARING_SRV
FAR_MANUAL_CLEARING_SRV

Everything is working again. Great.

Step 4 – Dump Occurring the Next Day

The next day, consultants reported dumps in the following transactions:

F-44 and ACACPOSTINGRUN

Dump reason:
SYNTAX_ERROR

The details stated:
The data object "BSEG" does not have a component called "ZZ*"

This means the system was still expecting the old ZZ field.

Solution:
To fix this issue, I ran the following program:

RGUGBR00 → Application Area = FI

After this, the dump issue was resolved.

Current Status:
Two days have passed since the deletion.
No new problems so far 🙂

Final Thoughts:
Changes to core structures like CI_COBL are very risky.

For this reason:
Do not apply functional requests immediately.
Always question if they are really needed.
Never do this in production without planning.

See you next time,
aATAN

entry.title: API to Read Change Entity Type in Dynamic BADI
entry.content:
Hi Team,

We have a requirement for Dynamic Agent in the CR update scenario. We need to determine whether the Company Code and/or Sales-level entity data has changed.

  • If only Company Code and/or Sales-level data is changed, the workflow should proceed with auto-approval.
  • If any General-level data changes along with Company Code/Sales, the workflow should route for one-step approval.

Need a logic or API to read change entity type because in read change document we only getting fieldname.

entry.title: 🇺🇸 Getting started with Generative AI Hub on SAP AI Core (Atlanta, GA, USA)
entry.content:
This SAP CodeJam event is on Getting Started with Generative AI Hub on SAP AI Core and Python. You do not need any background in the topic, but you should have a lot of curiosity!

In this CodeJam, you will learn how to use the Generative AI Hub on SAP AI Core to implement retrieval-augmented generation (RAG). This approach improves the responses of large language models (LLMs) and reduces hallucinations. You will learn how to deploy an LLM and the orchestration service on SAP AI Core, and how to query it via SAP AI Launchpad and the Python SDK. Additionally, you will gain insights into key generative AI concepts and learn how to enhance RAG responses.

Location and Date:

This is a free in-person event only (not virtual) and is planned for Thursday, February 26th, 2026 from 07:00 - 17:00 hrs  local time in Atlanta, GA, USA. The language of the event and the content will be English.

Event Address: 
Georgia Chapter Spring Meeting and Social - February 2026
Microsoft Atlantic Yards located at 170 17th Street Northwest, Atlanta, Georgia 30363

The instructor is SAP Developer Advocate @thomas_jung 

Prerequisites: 

  • Bring your own laptop with power adapter and have a supported Web browser such as Google Chrome or Firefox installed on your laptop.

Please note that spaces are limited! We can only accommodate 40 attendees. If you want to attend, register as part of the ASUG Chapter meeting event website: Georgia Chapter Spring Meeting and Social - February 2026 | ASUG

For more information on SAP CodeJam events, head over to the SAP CodeJam group.

entry.title: Strategic Anatomy: The Architecture of Automatic Account Determination.
entry.content:
Date: February 2026

Author:-  Er. Pratik Das (B.Tech, Mechanical Engineering | SAP Functional Consultant).

Target Audience: SAP Functional Consultants (MM,FI), Solution Architects, Financial Controllers & Business Process Owners (BPO), SAP S/4HANA Migration Leads & etc.

Strategic Anatomy: The Architecture of Automatic Account Determination.

  • Overview :-

In the SAP ecosystem, the integration between Materials Management and Financial Accounting is not merely a set of table entries; it is a sophisticated hierarchical logic designed to ensure fiscal integrity across global operations. At the architect level, understanding this interface requires a deep dive into the four-pillar dependency model: Account Category Reference, Valuation Class, Valuation Grouping Code, and Transaction Event Keys.

When these elements are synchronized, the system achieves a "Zero-Touch" accounting environment where logistics movements drive real-time financial reporting.

The Logical Hierarchy of Account Determination

The bridge between a physical warehouse movement and a journal entry is built through a specific sequence of inheritance. The system resolves the destination G/L account by traversing the following architectural layers:

  1. The Material Abstraction: Account Category Reference (ACR)

The Account Category Reference serves as the primary decoupling layer. Rather than creating a rigid 1:1 relationship between Material Types (e.g., ROH, FERT) and Valuation Classes, SAP utilizes the ACR to provide flexibility.

  • The Role: It acts as a mandatory intermediary. A Material Type is assigned to an ACR, and the ACR is then assigned to a range of Valuation Classes.
  • Architectural Value: This allows multiple Material Types to share a common set of Valuation Classes, simplifying the OBYC matrix and ensuring consistency across different procurement streams.
  1. The Financial Categorization: Valuation Class

The Valuation Class is the granular identifier maintained at the Material/Plant level. It is the primary vehicle that carries the logistics data into the financial world.

  • Strategic Function: It categorizes materials for accounting purposes without requiring a unique G/L for every single material number. For instance, all "Raw Materials - Steel" might be assigned to Valuation Class 3000, which the OBYC table then maps to a specific Inventory G/L.
  • Dependency: A Valuation Class can only be assigned to a material if its Material Type is linked to the same Account Category Reference.

III. The Organizational Grouping: Valuation Grouping Code (VGC)

For global rollouts, the Valuation Grouping Code (active via the Valuation Grouping Code switch) is essential for scaling.

  • The Problem: Without VGC, every Plant (Valuation Area) would need its own explicit entries in the OBYC table.
  • The Solution: VGC allows you to group multiple Plants together. If ten plants in the same region share the same Chart of Accounts and accounting logic, they are assigned to the same VGC (e.g., 0001).
  • The Impact: This reduces the volume of maintenance in the account determination table and allows for "Regionalized Accounting" where different groups of plants can hit different sets of G/L accounts for the same material movements.

The Engine Room: Transaction Event Keys and Account Modifiers

Once the organizational and material frameworks are defined, the Transaction Event Key acts as the execution trigger. These keys are hard-coded into the SAP kernel and are called based on the movement type or transaction code.

  1. BSX (Inventory Posting)

The BSX key is the anchor for all Balance Sheet inventory accounts. It is triggered by any valuated stock movement (e.g., Movement Type 101, 201, 301).

  • Critical Constraint: BSX does not support Account Modifiers. The mapping is a direct link between the VGC + Valuation Class and the Inventory G/L.
  1. GBB (Offsetting Entries)

Where BSX handles the inventory side of a posting, GBB handles the "other side" of the entry. Due to the vast array of possible offsetting scenarios, GBB utilizes General Modifications (Account Modifiers) to branch the logic:

  • VBR (Consumption): Diverts costs to expense accounts when goods are issued to Cost Centers or Orders.
  • BSA (Initial Load): Manages the offsetting entry for inventory gains during legacy data migration.
  • VBO (Subcontracting): Manages the consumption of components provided to a vendor.
  1. WRX (GR/IR Clearing)

The WRX key manages the liability accrual. It ensures that the system tracks "Goods Received but Not Yet Invoiced." This key is unique because it must bridge the valuation at the time of receipt with the final invoice price, often interacting with PRD (Price Difference) keys if variances occur under a Standard Price regime.

The Integration Bridge: Convergence in OBYC

The final convergence occurs in the T030 table (viewed via OBYC). The system's search string for a G/L account follows this precise technical path:

  1. Identify Transaction Key from the Movement Type (e.g., GBB).
  2. Determine Account Modifier if applicable (e.g., VBR).
  3. Locate Valuation Grouping Code of the Plant (from OMWD).
  4. Extract Valuation Class from the Material Master.
  5. Match these four variables against the OBYC matrix to retrieve the G/L Account.

Conclusion

A high-level SAP MM configuration is not about populating tables; it is about designing a scalable data flow. By correctly leveraging the Account Category Reference to group Material Types and the Valuation Grouping Code to group Plants, a consultant can maintain a lean and audit-ready FI-MM interface.

entry.title: Re: Can AS2 Sender iFlow be exposed with Basic Authentication via API Management?
entry.content:
Hi @aditya_20 

1. Can an AS2 sender based iFlow be fronted by APIM? 
Ans. No, APIM are exposing HTTP based iflows, not with other sender adapters e.g. AS2 and rather synchronous Iflows (with business response, not with technical ack)

2. If yes, can Basic Authentication be applied via API policies for AS2 sender iFlow?

Ans. If you want to create Iflow with HTTP sender adapter (replacing AS2) as in your cited blog, then of course it is possible also to protect API proxy by Basic Auth (instead of Oauth Client credentials):
https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-q-a/sap-api-management-set-basic-authentication-for-api-proxy-in-integration/qaq-p/13957841 

Just use KVM for user + password storage and then compare with those coming from Authorization header.

two points:
- Honestly I do not see any value added for creation of API proxy to http Iflow (what is described in above blog). Http iflow can be exposed in secure manner directly to external sender system without API proxy layer. If this is only purpose of creating API (no other policy steps that are unique for APIM, not available in CI).

- In CI you can secure access via your suggested Basic Auth as well.

3. If not, what are the supported and recommended security mechanism for inbound AS2 in this scenario?
Ans. it was mentioned in comments of cited blog. Client certificate authentication is native for AS2.

hope it helps

Mateusz

entry.title: Item text is missing in confirmation of requisition
entry.content:
Previously in the items data, business user can still add item text on it but now there is no field for it when we edit it to confirm/create the goods receipt for the PR/PO

 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Item text is missing in confirmation of requisition
entry.content:
Hi,

The Item Text field behavior is driven by document lifecycle and app design:

  • Item text can be entered or edited during PR or PO creation.

  • During Goods Receipt confirmation, the GR apps are designed primarily for logistics execution and do not allow editing of PR/PO item texts.

  • Once the PO is created (or in some cases approved), item texts become read-only and are no longer editable in follow-on documents like GR.

Therefore, the absence of the Item Text field during GR creation is standard system behavior in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition and not related to situation handling, notification templates, or text configuration.

If there is a business requirement to capture additional information at GR time, alternative options such as:

  • GR header/item notes (where available),

  • Attachments,

  • Or custom fields (via key user extensibility), can be evaluated.

Thanks and regards,

SAP Support

entry.title: Convite para Palestrantes – SAP Inside Track Belo Horizonte 2026
entry.content:
A Comunidade SAP Belo Horizonte está com o Call for Speakers aberto para o SAP Inside Track Belo Horizonte, que acontecerá no dia 21 de março.

O SAP Inside Track é um evento feito pela comunidade SAP e para a comunidade SAP.

Isso significa que procuramos pessoas que vivem SAP no dia a dia, com vontade de compartilhar experiências reais. Não importa se você é júnior, pleno ou sênior.

Se você já:

  • participou de projetos SAP

  • enfrentou desafios técnicos ou funcionais

  • aprendeu algo importante “na prática”

  • quer compartilhar um case, uma lição ou uma visão

👉 você já tem uma palestra.

 

Que tipo de sessão você pode submeter?

Estamos abertos a diferentes formatos, como:

  • palestras técnicas

  • demonstrações práticas

  • sessões mais leves ou conversas guiadas

  • mentorias em grupos menores

  • atividades livres e interativas

Os temas podem envolver (mas não se limitam a):
S/4HANA, BTP, Fiori, ABAP, ABAP Cloud, CDS Views, Integrações, Carreira SAP, Clean Core, RAP, experiências de projeto, entre outros.

 

Como funciona a inscrição?

Para se candidatar como palestrante, basta preencher o formulário de submissão, onde pedimos informações como:

  • seu perfil e especialidade

  • título e descrição da sessão

  • público-alvo e nível esperado

  • formato, duração e necessidades da sala

  • disponibilidade de horários

  • interesse em repetir a sessão ou atuar como mentor

Essas informações nos ajudam a montar uma agenda equilibrada, diversa e de alto valor para a comunidade.

👉 Link para o formulário de submissão:
CLIQUE AQUI - LINK DO FORMULÁRIO

 

Por que palestrar no SAP Inside Track?

  • Compartilhar conhecimento com a comunidade

  • Ganhar visibilidade profissional

  • Conectar-se com outros profissionais SAP

  • Fortalecer o ecossistema local de Belo Horizonte

O SAP Inside Track não é sobre palco — é sobre troca.

Se você sente que tem algo a contribuir, inscreva sua sessão.
E se conhecer alguém que deveria palestrar, marque essa pessoa aqui nos comentários.

Vamos construir juntos o SAP Inside Track Belo Horizonte 🚀

entry.title: SAP Analytics Cloud: Best Practices for Folder Organization and Governance
entry.content:
Introduction

In SAP Analytics Cloud projects, a significant amount of effort is spent on building models, stories, and planning applications. However, one critical topic is often underestimated: how these objects are organized. In many customer environments, reports and models are scattered across different folders without a clear structure, making it difficult to control access, ensure consistency, and maintain content over time. This is not just a usability issue, it is a governance challenge. A well-designed folder structure is a foundational element for scalable, secure, and maintainable SAC landscapes.

 

Why Folder Organization Matters in SAC?

Folder structure is not a cosmetic choice, it is a governance decision.

Number of objects like Stories, Models, Themes, Insights are rapidly increasing in SAC environments. It is crucial to keep these objects in organized way. Otherwise, this can cause important security and governance issues. Without well-planned folder structure,  important objects like corporate stories, models etc. can be located in My Files or Public Folders with ambiguous ownerships. It is not certain that who is using these files and folders and who are the owners. Without discrimination of folders, contents that should be tested first can be accidently used with Production purposes. Also with authorization complexity, unnecessary accesses can cause security issues. As a result, weak folder structure leads low maintainability, low security and high operational risk.

 

Core Folder Types

Understanding the core folder types in SAP Analytics Cloud is essential for building a sustainable governance model. Each folder type serves a specific purpose, and misusing them often leads to security risks, ownership issues, and long-term maintenance problems. The two primary folder types My Files and Public should be clearly differentiated and used intentionally.

My Files – Personal Workspace

My Files is designed as a personal working area for individual users in SAP Analytics Cloud. Content stored in My Files is accessible only by the owner and is not governed by role-based access control, which makes it suitable for early-stage development, drafts, experiments, and personal analysis. It serves as a sandbox environment where users can prototype ideas or perform temporary testing without impacting shared content. However, because objects in My Files are not centrally visible or governed unless explicitly shared, they are not appropriate for long-term or productive use.

As a best practice, any object that is actively used by business users should not located in My Files.

Public – Enterprise Content Area

Public is the central, governed content area in SAP Analytics Cloud and serves as the foundation for enterprise analytics. Unlike personal folders, content stored in Public is managed through role-based access control, enabling controlled visibility, clear ownership, and secure collaboration across teams and departments. Public folders support structured hierarchies, which makes them ideal for implementing environment separation such as DEV, TEST, and PROD, as well as department-based organization. All shared stories, models, planning applications, and related objects intended for long-term use should reside in the Public folder structure. By enforcing consistent naming conventions and access rules within Public, organizations can significantly improve maintainability, reduce operational risk, and establish a scalable governance model for SAP Analytics Cloud.

As a best practice, all productive SAC content should be located in the Public folder hierarchy.

 

DEV – TEST – PROD Folder Strategy

A clear DEV–TEST–PROD folder strategy is one of the most critical elements of governance in SAP Analytics Cloud. Separation  between development - testing and production provides a strong folder discipline. Especially, the separation is crucial if you are in a single tenant landscape. Even in multi-tenant landscapes (Dev-Test-Prod), this is also recommended since the transportation between tenants is not mandatory and system-driven. 

A structured folder strategy introduces a clear content lifecycle, reduces operational risk, and creates transparency for both IT and business users.

Recommended High-Level Folder Structure

/Public
├─ _DEV 
├─ _TEST
└─ _PROD

Environment Responsibilities

DEV

  • Active development and frequent changes
  • Broad access for developers and consultants
  • Experimental and incomplete content allowed

TEST

  • Business validation and user acceptance testing
  • Limited write access
  • Focus on functional correctness and data validation

PROD

  • Approved, production-ready content only
  • Strict access control
  • Stable objects consumed by business users

Object Type-Based Folder Design

Standardized subfolder design based on object types is a key enabler for consistency and scalability in SAP Analytics Cloud. By structuring folders according to object types—such as Stories, Models, Analytic Applications, Data Actions, and Allocations—organizations create a predictable and intuitive navigation experience across DEV, TEST, and PROD environments.

Department-Based Folder Design

By grouping stories, models, and applications under department-specific folders such as Finance, Sales, Supply Chain, or HR, ownership and accountability become immediately clear. This approach simplifies access management through role-based authorization, reduces the risk of unintended data exposure, and improves discoverability for business users. When combined with a DEV–TEST–PROD structure, department-based folders ensure that each team works within a clearly defined and governed space, supporting scalability and long-term maintainability of the SAC environment.

 

Workspaces

Workspaces in SAP Analytics Cloud are designed to support collaboration by grouping content, users, and access rights within a shared space. They can be useful for project-based or temporary initiatives where multiple users work closely on the same set of stories or models.

However, from a governance perspective, I personally avoid using Workspaces as a primary content container. When a Workspace is deleted, all content stored within it is permanently removed, which introduces a significant operational risk. For this reason, Workspaces should be used selectively and primarily for short-lived collaboration, while long-term and productive content should always reside in the Public folder structure.

You can navigate to this blog post by @ninak1 to learn workspaces.

 

Authorization Best Practices (Team-Based Governance)

Effective authorization in SAP Analytics Cloud should be managed through teams, not individual users. Assigning access directly to users quickly becomes unmanageable as environments grow, leading to inconsistent permissions, security risks, and high maintenance effort. A team-based authorization model provides scalability, transparency, and long-term governance by clearly separating responsibilities and standardizing access patterns.

Recommended Team Personas

A clear persona model is essential for aligning technical permissions with business responsibilities. The following team structure covers most SAC scenarios while remaining simple and scalable:

Admin

  • System and tenant administration
  • User, team, and role management
  • Full access across DEV, TEST, and PROD

Developer

  • Model, story, and application development
  • Full write access in DEV
  • Limited or no write access in PROD

Editor / Analyst 

  • Ability to create own stories and perform small adjustments
  • Typically business power users or advanced analysts
  • Write access to specific folders, without system-level permissions

Why “Editor / Analyst”?
Compared to “Developer,” this role better reflects business-driven content creation and controlled flexibility without exposing technical or administrative capabilities.

Viewer

  • Read-only access to validated, productive content
  • No content creation or modification rights

Folder Authorization via Teams

Folders should always be shared with teams, not individual users. This approach ensures that access rights are role-based, auditable, and easy to maintain.

Best Practices

  • DEV folders → Developer team (edit), Admin team (full)
  • TEST folders → Developer / Editor (limited edit), Viewer (read)
  • PROD folders → Viewer (read), Editor (controlled edit), Admin (full)

By assigning folder permissions at the team level, access changes are handled simply by adding or removing users from teams—without touching folder settings.

Role Assignment Strategy

In addition to folder access, SAC roles define what users can do at the platform level. These roles should also be assigned to teams rather than individuals.

Example Role Mapping

  • Admin team → BI Admin role
  • Developer team → Content Creator role
  • Editor / Analyst team → Content Creator (restricted by folder access)
  • Viewer team → Viewer role

Best Practice:
Assign roles and folder permissions to teams—not to individual users.

Here is the link of the detailed blog post for Security Concept by @Matthew_Shaw

 

What to Do Next

To apply these best practices in your SAP Analytics Cloud environment, start with the following steps:

  • Define your governance model first
    Agree on how content should be developed, validated, and consumed before creating folders.

  • Separate DEV, TEST, and PROD clearly
    Create dedicated environment folders under Public and enforce a forward-only content flow.

  • Organize content by department and object type
    Use department-based folders combined with standardized subfolders (Stories, Models, Data Actions, etc.).

  • Establish a clear naming convention
    Document and communicate a simple, consistent naming standard for all SAC objects.

  • Adopt team-based authorization
    Create teams based on personas (Admin, Developer, Editor/Analyst, Viewer) and assign both roles and folder permissions to teams—not individual users.

  • Review and clean up existing content
    Identify unused, duplicated, or misplaced objects and move productive content into the governed Public structure.

 

Sources

  • https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blog-posts-by-sap/sap-analytics-cloud-content-organization-and-authorization/ba-p/13350602
  • https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blog-posts-by-sap/sap-analytics-cloud-security-concepts-and-best-practice/ba-p/13416999
  • https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blog-posts-by-members/sap-analytics-cloud-workspaces/ba-p/13531009

Aziz Khan

SAP Digital Manufacturing SAP Digital Manufacturing Insights

entry.title: Re: Request for CDS View with Line Item Data of Recurring...
entry.content:
Dear @S0027269767,

In Public Cloud recurring journal entries are handled as a template object that later generates normal posted journal entries when you run/trigger posting. SAP delivers I_RecurringJournalEntry for the template header but what’s currently documented/released for Public Cloud there is no released CDS interface view that exposes the template’s line items

More details below:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0fa84c9d9c634132b7c4abb9ffdd8f06/ca03bbfc892d4474be69934d6f5c783d.html

What to use instead:

If your reporting requirement is line items of the postings created from recurring runs then you should report on the posted journal entry line items, using the released Journal Entry item views:

  • I_JournalEntryItem released interface view for journal entry item details (Universal Journal / ACDOCA-based)

More details below:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/c0c54048d35849128be8e872df5bea6d/47dce95760521070e10000000a44147b.html

  • I_JournalEntryItemCube released cube for journal entry item analytics in Public Cloud packages

More details below:

https://api.sap.com/cdsviews/I_JOURNALENTRYITEMCUBE

  • C_JournalEntryItemBrowser released consumption view used by the Display Line Items in General Ledger app, good confirmation it’s the standard line-item dataset in Public Cloud

More details below:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0fa84c9d9c634132b7c4abb9ffdd8f06/15ee31fac4be4e6497a76abe449fe49c.html

How to tie it back to recurring

1. Use Manage Recurring Journal Entries to trigger due postings and review posting status/results this is where you identify which accounting documents were generated from which recurring template

More details below:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0fa84c9d9c634132b7c4abb9ffdd8f06/114f17567f98692de10000000a4450e5.html

2. Then read the line items for those generated accounting documents from I_JournalEntryItem / I_JournalEntryItemCube / C_JournalEntryItemBrowser using the standard keys, typically Company Code + Fiscal Year + Accounting Document + Item

The generation step is also reflected in the standard job Post Recurring Journal Entries, which posts the due recurring originals into normal accounting documents

More details below:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0fa84c9d9c634132b7c4abb9ffdd8f06/cd189f57e7356d10e10000000a441470.html

Summary:

If you need template line items before posting in Public Cloud there isn’t a released CDS view that I can point you to for recurring-template items. Practically that means:

  • adjust the requirement to report on posted results recommended / stable via the views above

Best Regards,

Dawid

entry.title: How to extend the program SAPF124 of F.13 to retrieve its results
entry.content:
• Introduction
  • Selection screen parameters and result example
  • SAPF124 logic
  • Subroutine ECHTLAUF with I_KOART being 'D', 'K' or 'S'
  • Subroutine SPERREN_KONTO (lock)
  • Subroutine AUSGLEICH with I_SUM = sum of amounts of all documents with the same clearing criteria
  • Subroutine ACC_APPEND_DATA
  • Enhancements
  • Basic code to run SAPF124
  • ABAP type for the collected data

Introduction

F.13 is the transaction to clear the open items automatically. For functional information, see Automatic Clearing | SAP Help Portal.

The result is a spool which contains a log with all results.

To analyze the results by program, it's easier to create a few enhancements to F.13 (program SAPF124) to retrieve the log as several internal tables, rather than decoding the spool (called "ABAP List" hereafter), because the latter is often subject to changes after an upgrade.

Note that some people recommend in the forum to use POSTING_INTERFACE_CLEARING but I couldn't find any official information saying that it's allowed for automating F.13.

Before creating the enhancements to the program SAPF124, it's important to understand the logic of the program. Also, the names of some of the selection screen parameters and an example of the output ABAP List are useful.

Warning: in this blog post, I don't post the code I used, but I explain which subroutines can be enhanced.

 

Selection screen parameters and result example

Example of the output ABAP List. Here F.13 ran in test mode so there's no clearing document created (so, the number remains blank). We can see the list of cleared documents at the top, then the additional log and the error log at the bottom.

 

SAPF124 logic

AT SELECTION-SCREEN:

  • Call the subroutine CHECK_ECHTL
    • In productive mode (X_TESTL not checked), it displays the warning FG013 “This program run is a production run” as a popup.
    • It also calculates X_ECHTL = “X” or “ ”.
      • “X” means that the execution is in productive mode (X_TESTL not checked).

 

START-OF-SELECTION:

  • Call the subroutine KONTOTABELLEN_FUELLEN
    • Fills the 3 lists of objects to process: customers (internal table TDEBI), suppliers (internal table TKREDI) and accounts (internal table TSAKO), according to the selection screen parameters, based on the tables BSIK, BSIS and SKB1 respectively.
  • Logic for the 3 lists
    • Call the subroutine DEBI_VERARBEITEN if the "Customers" box is checked
      • LOOP AT TDEBI (customers)
        • Call subroutine ECHTLAUF with KOART = 'D'
    • Call the subroutine KREDI_VERARBEITEN if the "Suppliers" box is checked
      • LOOP AT TKREDI (suppliers)
        • Call subroutine ECHTLAUF with KOART = 'K'
    • Call the subroutine SAKO_VERARBEITEN if the "Accounts" box is checked
      • LOOP AT TSAKO (accounts)
        • Call subroutine ECHTLAUF with KOART = 'S'

 

Event END-OF-SELECTION:

  • Call the subroutine ACC_AUSGABE_ADDITION_LOG
    • Write the internal table IPROT (additional log) to the ABAP List.
  • IF X_FEHLER = 'X':
    • Call the subroutine ACC_AUSGABE_FAULT_LOG
      • It writes the internal table FEHLPROT (error messages) to the ABAP List.

 

Subroutine ECHTLAUF with I_KOART being 'D', 'K' or 'S'

  • For information, depending on I_KOART being 'D', 'K' or 'S', the data will be taken respectively from TDEBI, TKREDI or TSAKO.
  • Call subroutine SPERREN_KONTO to lock the object to process
  • Call subroutine INDEXTABELLEN_FUELLEN
    • READ_BSID or READ_BSIK or READ_BSIS to build the groups of clearing criteria XBSIDGR or XBSIKGR or XBSISGR, depending on I_KOART = 'D', 'K' or 'S' respectively.
      • APPEND XBSID or XBSIK or XBSIS
      • COLLECT XBSIDGR or XBSIKGR or XBSISGR depending on I_KOART = 'D', 'K' or 'S' respectively:
        • Each line is a group of clearing criteria for which FI documents may be cleared altogether.
        • The field XBSIDGR-BET_BW or XBSIKGR-BET_BW or XBSISGR-BET_TW contains the sum of FI documents which can be zero (they can be cleared) or another number (they cannot be cleared).
    • LOOP AT XBSIDGR or XBSIKGR or XBSISGR depending on I_KOART = 'D', 'K' or 'S' respectively
      • Call subroutine AUSGLEICH USING I_SUM = XBSIDGR-BET_BW or XBSIKGR-BET_BW or XBSISGR-BET_TW

 

Subroutine SPERREN_KONTO (lock)

  • If KOART = 'D', call ENQUEUE_EFKNB1A with tdebi-bukrs, tdebi-kunnr.
  • If KOART = 'K', call ENQUEUE_EFLFB1A with tkredi-bukrs, tkredi-lifnr.
  • If KOART = 'S', call ENQUEUE_EFSKB1A with tsako-bukrs, tsako-hkont, tsako-hbkid, tsako-hktid.

 

Subroutine AUSGLEICH with I_SUM = sum of amounts of all documents with the same clearing criteria

It checks if the clearing of one group of documents with the same clearing criteria is possible. If yes, it’s calling a given transaction code (whose code may vary) via Batch Input to create one clearing document.

The subroutine has the following logic:

  • Call the subroutine BELEGE_AUSGEBEN (collect the list of documents to be cleared)
    • Loop at all the lines of XBSID, XBSIK or XBSIS corresponding to the current grouping criteria XBSIDGR, XBSIKGR or XBSISGR depending on KOART = 'D', 'K' or 'S' respectively.
      • Call the subroutine RECORD_DOCUMENT
        • APPEND GS_DLIST TO GT_DLIST. It's the list of FI documents corresponding to the current clearing criteria group.
  • If I_SUM = 0 (the documents of the current group have a total amount of 0, so they can be cleared).
    • Call the subroutine AUGDT_PRUEFEN
      • Call the subroutine CALL_TRANSACTION if it's a productive run
        • Batch input CALL TRANSACTION FB1D, FB1K or FB1S/FB1SL depending on KOART = 'D', 'K' or 'S' respectively.
        • The batch input messages are stored into the internal table MESSTAB
        • Call the subroutine MESSTAB_AUSWERTEN
          • If MESSTAB contains the success message F5312 "Document & was posted in company code &", AUGBLNR (global variable) = MSGV1 (document number)
          • Call the subroutine RECORD_CLEARING_INFO with I_CHAR = 'AUGBL'
            • Call subroutine ACC_APPEND_DATA with parameters AUGBLNR, clearing date and ID_MESSAGE = ‘AUGBL’ to display the documents to be cleared and the clearing document created.
            • IMPORTANT: GT_DLIST is cleared (the list of documents to be cleared). 
              • Call subroutine RECORD_CLEARING_INFO with I_CHAR = code expressing errors (‘NOCLEAR’, ‘NOTZERO’, etc.)
          • If X_FEHLER = 'X'.
            • Call subroutine MESSTAB_LESEN which feeds the internal table FEHLPROT (displayed error log) from MESSTAB (error messages from the batch input)
  • Else if clearing is permitted with tolerance
    • TODO
  • Else (if no clearing)
    • Call subroutine ACC_APPEND_DATA with parameter ID_MESSAGE = ‘NOTZERO’.
    • If there’s some clearing tolerance, add the error to the internal table IPROT (additional log).

 

Subroutine ACC_APPEND_DATA

It receives the parameters:

  • ID_AUGBLNR = clearing document created
  • ID_AUGDT = clearing date
  • ID_MESSAGE = ‘AUGBL’ (clearing) or others (no clearing), etc.
    • AUGBL: The clearing has been done, i.e. the clearing document has been created, the global variable AUGBL contains this clearing document, the log contains F5312 (*).
    • NOTZERO: the clearing is not possible because the sum of amounts of the documents is not zero.
    • AUGDT: Test mode successful (in test mode, the F5312 and F5263 (*) messages are absent and there is no error).
    • NOCLEAR: The reconciliation document has failed (the F5312 (*) message is absent and no tolerance is requested or a tolerance is requested but F5263 is absent).
    • NOCLEARTOL: Failed (the F5312 (*) message is absent and a tolerance is requested and F5263 is present).
    • ENQ: failed because the object of the current clearing criteria could not be locked
    • Other values: Failed
    • (*) Batch Input may return one of the following messages:
      • F5312: "Document & was posted in company code &"
      • F5263: "The difference is too large for clearing"

This subroutine has the following logic:

  • Call the subroutine WRITE_LISTE if the output of cleared/clearing documents has been requested in the selection screen. This subroutine does:
    • LOOP AT GT_DLIST INTO GS_DLIST (list of documents to be cleared)
      • The document is written to the ABAP List.
    • Call subroutine CHECK_SUMME
      • The clearing criteria are written to the ABAP List on one line, including the new clearing document if one has been created.
  • GT_DLIST is emptied (the list of documents to be cleared).

 

Enhancements

The parameter X_FEHLER (to display error messages in the ABAP List) is absolutely necessary to run several SAPF124 subroutines which contain information about the cleared documents.

The following subroutines may be enhanced to obtain information about the cleared documents used and the clearing documents created:

  • Begin of CHECK_ECHTL
    • In real mode, it displays the warning FG013 “This program run is a production run”.
    • It also calculates X_ECHTL = “X” or “ ”. “X” means that the execution is in real mode (case F.13 is not called in test mode i.e., checkbox X_TESTL = space/false).
    • This message is annoying if SAPF124 is called by a custom program in a loop.
    • Do this enhancement:
      • Keep calculating X_ECHTL = “X” or “ ” but don’t display the warning by using ABAP “RETURN” to not execute the standard code of the subroutine.
  • Begin of ACC_APPEND_DATA
    • It’s called after the logic to create one clearing document, whatever it has succeeded (parameter ID_MESSAGE = ‘AUGBL’) or failed (other values).
    • Do this enhancement:
      • Collect the list of documents cleared (internal table GT_DLIST), the clearing status (ID_MESSAGE) and the clearing document created (ID_AUGBLNR). The clearing document will be empty if the creation failed.
  • Begin of MESSTAB_LESEN
    • It’s called when the creation of the cleared document failed via Batch Input and it feeds the internal table FEHLPROT from MESSTAB (error messages from Batch Input).
    • Do this enhancement:
      • Collect the list of documents cleared (internal table GT_DLIST) and the error messages from the internal table FEHLPROT.
  • Begin of ACC_AUSGABE_FAULT_LOG
    • It’s called at the end, but only if the selection screen option X_FEHLER was selected.
    • Do this enhancement:
      • Export the data previously collected by the enhancements to the ABAP memory (EXPORT TO MEMORY ID).

 

Basic code to run SAPF124

Transaction F.13 starts SAPF124 with the SAP&F124 variant, so I'm doing the same thing. I haven't checked to see if there's a difference without the variant.

The actual content is obtained by reading the data collected by the enhancements in the ABAP memory (IMPORT FROM MEMORY ID).

SUBMIT sapf124
    USING SELECTION-SET 'SAP&F124'
    WITH bukrx   IN ir_bukrs     " Company
    WITH gjahx   IN ir_gjahr     " Year
    WITH docnr   IN ir_belnr     " Document Number
    WITH x_kunnr  = iv_xkunnr    " Check "Customers"
    WITH kontd   IN ir_kunnr     " Customers
    WITH x_lifnr  = iv_xlifnr    " Check "Suppliers"
    WITH kontk   IN ir_lifnr     " Suppliers
    WITH x_saknr  = iv_xsaknr    " Check "Accounts"
    WITH konts   IN ir_saknr     " Accounts
    WITH x_testl  = iv_xtestl    " Test mode
    WITH xausbel  = abap_true    " Output the documents cleared
    WITH xnausbel = abap_true    " Output the documents not cleared
    WITH x_fehler = abap_true    " Output the error messages
    EXPORTING LIST TO MEMORY     " To not generate a spool
    AND RETURN.

 

ABAP type for the collected data

In my final code, a variable of type TT_CLEARING_RESULT is exported by the enhancement to the ABAP memory, and imported after SUBMIT SAPF124, which is defined as follows:

    TYPES:
      BEGIN OF ts_doc_item_to_be_cleared,
        bukrs TYPE bseg-bukrs,
        belnr TYPE bseg-belnr,
        gjahr TYPE bseg-gjahr,
        buzei TYPE bseg-buzei,
      END OF ts_doc_item_to_be_cleared.
    TYPES tt_doc_item_to_be_cleared TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF ts_doc_item_to_be_cleared WITH DEFAULT KEY.
    TYPES:
      BEGIN OF ts_clearing_doc_created,
        bukrs TYPE bseg-bukrs,
        belnr TYPE bseg-belnr,
        gjahr TYPE bseg-gjahr,
      END OF ty_ clearing_doc_created.
    "! Batch Input errors
    TYPES tt_journal_sapf124 TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF bdcmsgcoll WITH DEFAULT KEY.
    TYPES:
      BEGIN OF ts_clearing_result
        s_clearing_doc_created   TYPE ts_clearing_doc_created
        t_doc_item_to_be_cleared TYPE tt_doc_item_to_be_cleared,
        t_journal                TYPE tt_journal_sapf124,
      END OF ts_clearing_result.
    TYPES tt_clearing_result TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF ts_clearing_result WITH DEFAULT KEY.

Depending on the selection criteria when calling SAPF124, the result may contain many rows, some failing, others succeeding. For example, to clear 2 documents A 2024 and B 2025, which we know can be cleared, unfortunately the selection will be wide and there may be 4 documents selected: A 2024, A 2025, B 2024, B 2025.

In the result, there will likely be failures on A 2025 and B 2024, and a success for A 2024 and B 2025. Since we are only interested in the 2 documents A 2024 and B 2025, we must search the result for the row concerning them.

Example of collected data of type TT_CLEARING_RESULT :

Only the 6th line shows a success, the clearing document 0900002549 has been created to clear the documents shown in the component T_DOC_ITEM_TO_BE_CLEARED:

entry.title: Accelerate Your Role in SAP Supply Chain Transformation: Join Cloud Acceleration Partner Sessions
entry.content:
Today’s supply chains are evolving faster than ever—and SAP’s customers are moving with urgency to modernize both their manufacturing and planning landscapes. To help partners lead these critical transformations, SAP is offering two dedicated sessions that introduce the SAP Supply Chain Management Evolution Program, designed to equip you with the insight and confidence needed to guide customers through their next steps.

Why These Sessions Matter

Across manufacturing and planning, SAP customers are shifting from long‑established on‑premise solutions to the next generation of cloud‑based platforms:

  • SAP ME/MII → SAP Digital Manufacturing
  • SAP APO and ERP-based planning → SAP Integrated Business Planning (SAP IBP)

Both transformations represent major opportunities for partners. But they also require a clear understanding of the Customer Evolution Program—SAP’s structured approach to helping customers modernize with minimal risk and maximum long‑term value.

 What You’ll Gain by Attending

Each session is designed to give partners a practical understanding of:

  • How SAP is guiding customers through cloud transformation in manufacturing and planning
  • The purpose and structure of the SAP Supply Chain Management Evolution Program
  • The vital role partners play in helping customers adopt SAP Digital Manufacturing and SAP IBP
  • Real examples of customer transformation journeys and what drives success
  • Where partners can access resources, enablement, and next steps

By attending, you’ll leave with a clearer view of how to position the program with your customers—and how to identify opportunities to accelerate their transformation journey.

 Register for the Sessions

We encourage all partners supporting manufacturing and supply chain planning to join:

  • February 26 – SAP ME/MII → SAP Digital Manufacturing
  • March 4 – SAP APO/ERP → SAP Integrated Business Planning

These sessions are your opportunity to understand how SAP is helping customers evolve—and how you, as a partner, can play a central role in that journey.

entry.title: What’s New in SAP Cloud Identity Access Governance
entry.content:
Strengthening Access Visibility, Automation, and Governance

As organizations continue to modernize their system landscapes, access governance must adapt to increasing complexity. Managing users, roles, groups, and entitlements across hybrid and cloud environments calls for clearer visibility, smarter integration options, and dependable audit support.

The Q4 2025 updates to SAP Cloud Identity Access Governance (IAG) introduce a fresh set of enhancements spanning application integrations, APIs, job management, and reporting. These additions bring more flexibility, improved insight into access data, and expanded options for governing access. As a result, this helps teams work more efficiently as their environments grow and evolve.

Let’s take a closer look at what’s new in this release!

 

Seamless Application Integration with SAP HANA Cloud

SAP Cloud Identity Access Governance now supports deeper integration with SAP HANA Cloud, enabling synchronization of users, groups, and group authorizations. This integration lays the foundation for consistent access governance by supporting risk assessment, user provisioning with group assignments, and certification processes, all from a centralized governance layer.

Please refer to the following for more information: Help Portal Documentation

 

User Filter Support Across All Applications

User filter functionality is now extended to all SAP Cloud Identity Access Governance integration scenarios. This enhancement allows customers to exclude users who do not need to be governed, ensuring governance efforts remain focused and effective.

Please refer to the following for more information: Help Portal Documentation

 

Enhanced Access Request API for Greater Flexibility

The Access Request API has been enhanced by removing the mandatory domain field and introducing flexible user identification options. Users can now be retrieved using identifiers such as email, global user ID, or a universal search parameter.

Please refer to the following for more information: Help Portal Documentation

 

Manage Jobs - Change History Visibility

A new change history capability has been introduced in the Manage Jobs application. Administrators can now view updates made to jobs, including pause and resume actions, as well as the users responsible for those changes.

Please refer to the following for more information: Help Portal Documentation

 

Business Role Coverage Report

The new Business Role Coverage Report provides clear insights into how user roles are mapped within business roles. With smart filters, intuitive selection tools, and easy download options, administrators can quickly identify gaps and ensure proper role coverage.

Please refer to the following for more information: Help Portal Documentation

 

Unassociated Access Report

Embedded within the Business Coverage Report, the Unassociated Access Report helps administrators identify roles and groups that are not linked to any business roles. Users can drill down into details and refine searches using smart filters to pinpoint specific unassociated accesses.

Please refer to the following for more information: Help Portal Documentation

 

Access Report

The Access Report provides a comprehensive view of all users along with their assigned and unassigned accesses. It also shows how accesses relate to business roles, making it easier to validate access origins and appropriateness.

Please refer to the following for more information: Help Portal Documentation

 

Access Usage Report

The new Access Usage Report combines the functionality of the previously separate Unused Access and Actively Used Access reports into a single unified view. It supports on-premise and ABAP-based systems, including SAP HANA on-premise and SAP ERP.

Please refer to the following for more information: Help Portal Documentation

 

Closing Thoughts

These enhancements mark another strong step forward for SAP Cloud Identity Access Governance, bringing greater clarity, flexibility, and control to how organizations manage access across increasingly complex landscapes. From deeper integrations and APIs to unified reporting and stronger audit transparency, this release is designed to help teams govern access with confidence, efficiency, and precision.

As access governance continues to evolve alongside cloud and hybrid environments, SAP Cloud Identity Access Governance remains committed to delivering practical, scalable innovations that reduce risk, simplify operations, and support compliance at every stage. We’re excited to see how these capabilities help you strengthen your governance processes, and we look forward to continuing this journey with you as even more improvements arrive in upcoming releases.

For more details and configuration guidance, please refer to the SAP Help Portal documentation for SAP Cloud Identity Access Governance.

entry.title: Re: ABAP RAP - Action is not Updating the UI (unmanaged /...
entry.content:
@wjanes 

First, you need to update the entity by `MODIFY ENTITIES` statement.  After that, re-read the updated entity via `READ ENTITIES` and then pass the output to the result parameter.

Example form Help Portal:

  METHOD set_status_accepted.

    " Modify in local mode: BO-related updates that are not relevant for authorization checks
    MODIFY ENTITIES OF /DMO/I_Travel_M IN LOCAL MODE
           ENTITY travel
              UPDATE FIELDS ( overall_status )
                 WITH VALUE #( FOR key IN keys ( %tky      = key-%tky
                                                 overall_status = 'A' ) ). " Accepted

    " Read changed data for action result
    READ ENTITIES OF /DMO/I_Travel_M IN LOCAL MODE
      ENTITY travel
         ALL FIELDS WITH
         CORRESPONDING #( keys )
       RESULT DATA(travels).

    result = VALUE #( FOR travel IN travels ( %tky      = travel-%tky
                                              %param    = travel ) ).

  ENDMETHOD.

entry.title: Re: SAP Download Manager error (Unable to read basket con...
entry.content:
Regarding to your issue, please kindly note that SAP Download Manager does not work with multi-factor authentication (MFA) or SAP Universal ID (UID).

If your S-user is linked to UID, do not enter the UID password in SAP Download Manager. The UID password is the one you use to logon SAP for Me.

Instead, enter your individual S-user ID and Password as mentioned in KBA 3016110. 

If you do not remember the password of your S-user, kindly use below link to reset it. When reset your password, please input the S-user ID instead of email address:

Forgot My Password

Then you will use your download manager normally as before.

You can still use your UID password to logon SAP for Me.

entry.title: IBP-KF Calculation at aggregated Qty to a Specific Product
entry.content:
Hello,

I need expert guidance on a key figure (KF) calculation requirement.

We have a mother pack that consists of multiple SKUs, and there is an attribute called Mother Pack Active (Y/N). The requirement is to aggregate the Actuals Quantity of all related SKUs and copy the aggregated value into a new key figure where Mother Pack Active = 'Y'.

(all products sum into one product based on Active status)

Please let us know the best approach to achieve this in IBP.

Regards,

entry.title: Re: Will there be support for Crystal Reports Runtime 64...
entry.content:
Hello,

CR for VS follows the same time line as Crystal Reports Designer, so the end of life is typically a few years in the future.

CR products supports the current less 2 versions. And the dates are extended each time a new release is shipped.

CR for VS only has full versions available so always keep it up to date, typically 2 releases per year.

To many users, so don't expect CR or CR for VS ever to be discontinued, note CR for VS 32 bit is end of life now, only 64 bit is available in the new SP 40 release since there is no reason to support 32 bit applications anymore...

entry.title: new email address didn't receive UID verification code
entry.content:
Our new colleague wants to add her PERSON_NAME email address to her Univeral ID.
When she enters the email address no email with verification code is sent to this new email address.
How to go next?

Can anyone help here?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: new email address didn't receive UID verification code
entry.content:
Dear customer,

If user has not received an email with the verification code from noreply-uid@sap.com, could you pls kindly:

  • check and make sure if the entered email address is correct and valid. If not, enter the correct email address again
  • click 'Resend verification code' to request for a new verification code

This is documented in KBA 2948398 - Did not receive verification code email when setting up SAP Universal ID.

If still no any verification code sent to the email address, feel free to create case under component XX-SER-UID with the issue details.

Hope the above information helps.

Thank you and regards,

SAP Support

entry.title: How to re-generate license key for SAP BusinessObjects Business Intelligence platform
entry.content:
Hi team

We generate 200 license within one license key before, and there is no available material now.

can we revoke existing license key and generate new two license key with 100 number each ?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: How to re-generate license key for SAP BusinessObject...
entry.content:
Dear customer,

For BOBJ products, if the current license key quantity needs to be reduced, yes, kindly delete the existing license key, then the quantity 200 will be released back.

Then pls create a new license key with the proper number of quantity (e.g. 100) again.

This is documented in KBA 2446681 - Best practices of creating and updating SAP BusinessObjects license keys and system data - SAP for Me:
--------------------------------------------------------------------------

  • If it is required to reduce the quantity in an existing license key; delete the existing license key and then request a new license key for the correct quantity.
  • If it is required to increase the quantity in an existing license key, do one of the following:
    • Delete the existing license key and then request a new license key for the correct quantity
    • Request a license key for the additional quantity required as long as the total licenses per deployment complies with your license agreement

--------------------------------------------------------------------------

Hope this helps. 

Thanks and regards,
SAP Support

entry.title: How to hide fields /Tabs in SAP PPM?
entry.content:
1.- Right-click and then click on Technical Help.

 

2.- Click on the Component Configuration link

 

3.- Select the RIH_EDIT variant and choose CREATE CUSTOMIZING in the "Other Functions" drop-down menu.

 

4.- In Customizing Mode, you can hide elements. Select the views or subviews you want to hide and check in "Hidden Element" checkbox. E.g. Select the View "Deliverables", check in the "Hidden Element" and finally "Save" the changes.

 

5.- If you access the item you will see that the "Deliveries" tab does not appear.

You can create one customizing transport request and accomodate all the changes in a single TR
